{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Objective and Optimization {#sec-objective-optimization}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- Turning a model and a corpus into learned weights: the loss surface, the
     optimizer, and the schedules that actually make it work. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## The loss, revisited

<!-- Cross-entropy over the whole sequence in parallel (teacher forcing);
     from @sec-language-modelling to a concrete minibatch loss. -->

## Optimizers

<!-- SGD -> Adam -> AdamW; what the moment estimates buy; weight decay done
     right. -->

## Learning-rate schedules

<!-- Warmup + cosine/linear decay; why warmup; batch size interactions. -->

## Regularization and stability

<!-- Gradient clipping, dropout (or its absence), initialization. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
